title: The M.V.P.

jokes.text: What time did you need me to show up to watch you play the baseball?
jokes.setup: David treating baseball with formal distance despite dating Patrick
jokes.punchline: The awkward phrasing 'play the baseball' reveals David's complete unfamiliarity with sports
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific comedy - only David would phrase it this way, shows his privileged distance from common activities

jokes.text: Can you play both parts? / Positions. And, no.
jokes.setup: David uses theater terminology for sports
jokes.punchline: Patrick's gentle correction followed by matter-of-fact 'no'
jokes.explanation: Quick character beat showing David's theater background bleeding into sports context

jokes.text: Well on the upside, there is an Isabelle Huppert double feature playing at the Elmdale Art House.
jokes.setup: Patrick needs help with his baseball crisis
jokes.punchline: David immediately pivots to obscure French cinema as an alternative
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures David's pretentious cultural preferences and his desire to avoid sports at all costs

jokes.text: We just need a body! / Then go to the morgue.
jokes.setup: Patrick desperately explaining they just need a warm body
jokes.punchline: David's morbid literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Sharp, dark wit delivered with perfect deadpan timing - very quotable

jokes.text: I will cover you if any flies come your way. / I have a spray for that.
jokes.setup: Patrick offers protection from fly balls
jokes.punchline: David thinks he means actual insects
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke playing on David's literal interpretation and his preparedness for luxury problems

jokes.text: Given today's political climate, we don't need to divide ourselves any more than we already have.
jokes.setup: David explaining his stance on team sports
jokes.punchline: Treating recreational baseball as a divisive political issue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that perfectly captures David's overthinking and pseudo-intellectual approach to avoiding things

jokes.text: How many of the questions are about the barbecue? / Most of them.
jokes.setup: Patrick asking about David's questions
jokes.punchline: David's honest admission that he only cares about food
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - his priorities are completely food-focused despite the athletic context

jokes.text: I don't do well with running.
jokes.setup: David finally agreeing to play baseball
jokes.punchline: His immediate admission of physical limitations
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment showing David's self-awareness about his physical limitations

jokes.text: I can't stomach the thought of Patrick's team winning by default; the man looks like a thumb!
jokes.setup: Ronnie explaining why they need to play the game
jokes.punchline: Her oddly specific insult about Patrick's appearance
jokes.explanation: Hilarious and memorable visual insult that perfectly captures Ronnie's blunt personality and competitive spirit

jokes.text: There were seven of us, surprisingly, not a big turn-out, but uh, the Flying Latkes ended up in fourth place.
jokes.setup: Johnny elaborating on his Hebrew school baseball experience
jokes.punchline: The team name 'Flying Latkes' and finishing fourth out of presumably very few teams
jokes.explanation: The team name 'Flying Latkes' is brilliantly absurd and culturally specific, making fourth place even funnier

jokes.text: For one thing, the war is over. Ohhh!
jokes.setup: Roland dismissing Johnny's baseball experience
jokes.punchline: The insensitive war reference delivered with his typical crudeness
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate insensitivity from Roland, but relies on cheap stereotyping rather than clever writing

jokes.text: The Rabbi used to call me 'Popeye'.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to prove his athletic prowess
jokes.punchline: The unlikely nickname from a religious figure
jokes.explanation: Amusing juxtaposition of religious authority figure using cartoon nickname, sets up running gag

jokes.text: I'm gonna put everything I got on the other team.
jokes.setup: Learning Johnny will be on their team
jokes.punchline: Roland immediately switching his bet against his own team
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland logic - his gambling takes priority over team loyalty, very character-specific

jokes.text: I did have a cousin who was in 'Riverdance' until she got trampled.
jokes.setup: Twyla sharing her performance experience
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of getting trampled in an Irish dance show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Twyla moment - dark family history delivered with cheerful obliviousness

jokes.text: Yeah, um, I was one of the original Pussycat Dolls, But I only lasted like, two weeks. Because apparently, I'm 'too pretty'.
jokes.setup: Alexis sharing her performance background
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous claim about being too pretty for the Pussycat Dolls
jokes.explanation: Pure Alexis - an outrageous humblebrag delivered with complete sincerity, perfectly in character

jokes.text: I don't. / Fine, 'artist'.
jokes.setup: Question about Stevie wanting to be an actor
jokes.punchline: Stevie's flat denial followed by Alexis's attempted correction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Stevie's reluctance and Alexis trying to be encouraging

jokes.text: I can't think of anything worse than standing on a stage in front of a room full of people.
jokes.setup: Stevie explaining her stage fright
jokes.punchline: The irony that she's signed up to be the star of a play
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - Stevie expressing exactly what she's about to have to do

jokes.text: If this is anything like my first day with the Pussycat Dolls, it'll be a couple of hours of Kegels, and then an afternoon of cheeky Bellinis.
jokes.setup: Alexis trying to reassure Stevie about rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Her completely inappropriate comparison to pop star training
jokes.explanation: Builds perfectly on the Pussycat Dolls setup with hilariously specific and inappropriate details

jokes.text: Just like Nicole Scherzinger did for me.
jokes.setup: Alexis promising to help Stevie
jokes.punchline: Another layer to her Pussycat Dolls fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds another layer to Alexis's delusion while staying in character

jokes.text: Not now, not yet.
jokes.setup: Moira being theatrical with her Cabaret welcome
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic pause and direction to herself
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira theatricality - she can't help but direct even her own entrances

jokes.text: And I want to start the day with my lupanarian Kit Kat Girls!
jokes.setup: Moira addressing the female cast members
jokes.punchline: Her use of the unnecessarily elaborate word 'lupanarian' (meaning relating to a brothel)
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Moira - using an obscure and inappropriately sexual word in a completely innocent context

jokes.text: Missing cues already. No, I'm-I'm simply here to inspire.
jokes.setup: Someone suggests she's missing cues
jokes.punchline: Moira's defensive explanation that she's 'inspiring' not performing
jokes.explanation: Good Moira moment - her need to be above criticism while still being the center of attention

jokes.text: You're wondering, are we really going to lay this colossal enterprise upon her tiny shoulders? Yes!
jokes.setup: Moira introducing Stevie as the star
jokes.punchline: The over-dramatic description that emphasizes how unprepared Stevie seems
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment where Moira's theatrical confidence contrasts with obvious reality of the situation

jokes.text: I'm a 100% confident that you will all soon see what I hope I believe I may be seeing in you, Stevie.
jokes.setup: Moira expressing confidence in Stevie
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly uncertain language that undermines her stated confidence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant writing showing Moira's verbal sophistication masking her complete uncertainty - the language gets more tentative with each word

jokes.text: Oh, fuck.
jokes.setup: Learning about the 'Monster on my Back' exercise
jokes.punchline: Stevie's quiet dread delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie reaction - economical but expressive of her mounting horror at the situation

jokes.text: This glove is brown. My shoes are black. What exactly are we doing here?
jokes.setup: David's continued fashion concerns
jokes.punchline: His genuine distress over mismatched sporting goods
jokes.explanation: Continuation of David's misplaced priorities - treating color coordination as a genuine crisis

jokes.text: Well, David holds the Little League record for most times hit by a ball. That's assuming it wasn't intentional.
jokes.setup: Johnny sharing David's baseball history
jokes.punchline: The implication that David might have been deliberately targeted
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny revelation about David's childhood, with the perfect tag about it being intentional

jokes.text: I just wish I'd kept that helmet I got you.
jokes.setup: Johnny's continued concern for David's safety
jokes.punchline: Reference to special protective equipment David apparently needed
jokes.explanation: Good callback that builds on the previous revelation about David's baseball vulnerability

jokes.text: They let me go home early.
jokes.setup: David explaining his Little League experience
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact admission of being essentially expelled from children's sports
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of childhood athletic failure - David's ability to make trauma sound casual

jokes.text: Does this come in black?
jokes.setup: David looking at sports equipment
jokes.punchline: His continued obsession with color coordination even for safety gear
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag of David's fashion priorities in inappropriate situations

jokes.text: Once... Upon... A... Nightmare... My... Captor... Was... Dismembering...
jokes.setup: Word association exercise between director and star
jokes.punchline: The story escalating to extremely dark territory
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing how Moira and Stevie's energies combine to create something disturbing

jokes.text: I'm just flowing with your energy, Stevie. Which is good, but... you may require a little scordatura.
jokes.setup: Moira trying to explain the dark turn in their exercise
jokes.punchline: Her use of an obscure musical term (scordatura = unconventional tuning) as acting direction
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira vocabulary - using a complex musical term in completely inappropriate context while trying to be helpful

jokes.text: One, two. One, two. Two. Two... two, two.
jokes.setup: Moira trying to number off the group
jokes.punchline: Her inability to execute simple counting system
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment - Moira's directorial incompetence shown through basic task failure

jokes.text: I'm just wondering when the Bellinis are coming out?
jokes.setup: Alexis enduring the theatrical exercises
jokes.punchline: Her focus on the promised alcohol from her earlier Pussycat Dolls story
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Alexis actually believed her own fantasy about how rehearsals work

jokes.text: Okay, next time, can you make sure the ball goes into my glove? Thanks so much.
jokes.setup: David missing a catch during practice
jokes.punchline: His polite but unrealistic request for Patrick to make catching easier
jokes.explanation: Perfect David logic - politely requesting that physics work differently to accommodate his limitations

jokes.text: Nobody's gonna call you Popeye.
jokes.setup: Johnny referencing his old nickname
jokes.punchline: David's blunt assessment of current reality
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Johnny's nostalgia with David's characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: There are no sons in baseball!
jokes.setup: Johnny saying David is his son
jokes.punchline: Roland's dramatic declaration of family neutrality in sports
jokes.explanation: Hilariously overwrought declaration that perfectly captures Roland's melodramatic approach to competition

jokes.text: I'd argue most baseball players are somebody's son.
jokes.setup: Roland's dramatic 'no sons in baseball' statement
jokes.punchline: Patrick's logical deflation of the absurd premise
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response that uses simple logic to completely undermine Roland's dramatics

jokes.text: It's like when we used to play the Catholic kids from Our Lady of Perpetual Sorrow. The worst school cheer I ever heard.
jokes.setup: Patrick explaining sports rivalry
jokes.punchline: The school name 'Our Lady of Perpetual Sorrow' making for a depressing cheer
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay - Catholic school name inadvertently creates worst possible sports cheer

jokes.text: It's like when we used to play the Catholic kids from Our Lady of Perpetual Sorrow. The worst school cheer I ever heard.
jokes.setup: Patrick explaining rivalry in sports
jokes.punchline: The name 'Our Lady of Perpetual Sorrow' being inherently depressing for a cheer
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Catholic school naming creating inherently gloomy cheers

jokes.text: I'm wearing my maroon plaid hoodie. And I'm feeling... very uncomfortable.
jokes.setup: Acting exercise asking Stevie to recall a traumatic memory
jokes.punchline: Her description of the current moment rather than a memory
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy where Stevie uses the exercise to express her actual discomfort with the situation

jokes.text: There's that razor sharp wit that landed you the role.
jokes.setup: Stevie's honest admission about not doing the exercise
jokes.punchline: Moira misinterpreting Stevie's literal honesty as clever acting
jokes.explanation: Good irony as Moira completely misreads Stevie's straightforward discomfort as artistic brilliance

jokes.text: Okay, but we've chatted 30 seconds already, so we'll call it a company four-and-a-half.
jokes.setup: Moira granting a five-minute break
jokes.punchline: Her immediate micro-management of break time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira control-freak behavior - even break time must be precisely managed

jokes.text: If only we needed to fill the role of a Dictaphone!
jokes.setup: Moira dismissing Alexis's concerns about Stevie
jokes.punchline: Her use of an outdated office machine as an insult
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly weird Moira insult - using an obsolete recording device to suggest Alexis just repeats things mechanically

jokes.text: You should've seen the conditioning Rocky put me through. Our first day consisted of mud runs, and sob therapy!
jokes.setup: Moira defending her directing intensity
jokes.punchline: Claims her director training included 'sob therapy' along with physical conditioning
jokes.explanation: Great Moira invention - 'sob therapy' as part of director training perfectly matches her dramatic worldview

jokes.text: Okay, I don't know who this is, but can we put him back in the box?
jokes.setup: Johnny getting very intense during the game
jokes.punchline: David's disturbed reaction to his father's competitive transformation
jokes.explanation: Good visual of David's horror at seeing a different side of his usually mild father

jokes.text: There's basically nobody out there, Nobody's gonna catch it, go ahead.
jokes.setup: Ronnie encouraging her batter to hit to right field
jokes.punchline: Her loud declaration that David is incompetent while he can hear
jokes.explanation: Classic Ronnie bluntness creating cringe comedy as she insults David within earshot

jokes.text: Is-is there a bell I could ring?
jokes.setup: Bob struggling with charades during the theater game
jokes.punchline: His desperate request for an alternative to performance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Bob's discomfort with theatrical performance

jokes.text: A firefighter with back pain?
jokes.setup: Charades game during theater exercise
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific guess that reveals small-town knowledge
jokes.explanation: Small-town humor - everyone knows everyone's specific ailments

jokes.text: Oh, I actually forgot what I was supposed to do up here!
jokes.setup: Bob successfully guessing 'man with amnesia'
jokes.punchline: His immediate demonstration of the condition he just guessed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where Bob accidentally embodies the character he just identified - perfect structure and timing

jokes.text: Why? It's the only part of this stupid game that I'm actually good at.
jokes.setup: Patrick wanting to pinch-hit for David
jokes.punchline: David claiming batting is his strength after striking out three times
jokes.explanation: Good ironic self-assessment from David who completely lacks perspective on his athletic abilities

jokes.text: I tipped it that one time, you even admitted that!
jokes.setup: David defending his batting record
jokes.punchline: His pride in the minimal achievement of making contact once
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation of David's delusion about his abilities, treating a foul tip as a major accomplishment

jokes.text: David Rose, hit the ball!
jokes.setup: David actually getting a hit in the crucial moment
jokes.punchline: The celebration chant using his full formal name
jokes.explanation: Good payoff moment with the formal name making the small-town celebration more endearing

jokes.text: The next time I need a sub, remind me to ask the blind goat over at Mrs. Divine's farm.
jokes.setup: Roland bitter about losing the game
jokes.punchline: His comparison of Johnny to a visually impaired farm animal
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and mean-spirited Roland insult that perfectly captures his poor sportsmanship

jokes.text: Well, luckily for me, I had a side bet on whether Johnny would throw the game in his kid's favour. I consider it a split.
jokes.setup: Ronnie explaining her betting strategy
jokes.punchline: Her hedge bet that covers multiple outcomes
jokes.explanation: Smart callback showing Ronnie's gambling sophistication and her ability to find a win in any scenario

jokes.text: When you get hit in the back playing a game you never wanted to play in the first place, does that make you the VIP?
jokes.setup: David asking about MVP status after getting hurt
jokes.punchline: His confusion of MVP with VIP while seeking recognition for suffering
jokes.explanation: Perfect David logic - confusing sports terminology while seeking validation for his martyrdom

jokes.text: The MVP, and uh, no, we're not doing that.
jokes.setup: David's confused VIP question
jokes.punchline: Patrick's gentle correction followed by firm boundary-setting
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response showing Patrick's patience with David while not enabling his dramatics

jokes.text: Yeah, it was mainly because I was smelling the barbeque.
jokes.setup: Patrick suggesting David's competitive fire helped him
jokes.punchline: David's honest admission that food motivation was the real factor
jokes.explanation: Perfect final David moment - deflating any heroic interpretation with his characteristic honesty about his food priorities

tmdb_overview: David agrees to sub in for the annual baseball game, and Moira runs a rehearsal that pushes Stevie over the edge.
insight: Character comedy sustains a solid 75.6 despite absent standout jokes—consistency over peaks.
